revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:今尾秋葉神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} | image = {{Image needed|cat=Kaizu City}} | name = Imao Akiba Shrine | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} | location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|Kaizu|en|Kaizu|qid=Q844056|lt=Kaizu City|1=Kaizu}}{{ill|Hirata Town|ja|平田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Town|1=namedachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483353}}Imao | latitude = | longitude = | map_country_code = JP | map = Japan Gifu | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} | deity = {{ill|Akiba Daigongen|ja|秋葉大権現|1=Akiba Gongen|lt=Akiba Daigongen|3=秋葉権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiba Daigongen|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiba Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 秋葉権現}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} | type = | founded = {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|qid=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} 4 ({{ill|1767|en|1767|qid=Q7672|lt=1767|1=1767}}) | main_hall = | alternate_names = | tablet_sites = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} | festival = | ritual = Sagichō (February 11) }}
'''Imao Akiba Shrine''' (いまおあきばじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} {{ill|Kaizu|en|Kaizu|qid=Q844056|lt=Kaizu City|1=Kaizu}}. It is one of the {{ill|Akiba Shrines|ja|秋葉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiba Shrines|qq=}} – approximately 400 shrines under the umbrella of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|qid=Q908077|lt=Shrine Headquarters|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}} – spread across Japan. Recognized as one of Japan's three major Sagichō, the shrine is renowned for its magnificent fire festival {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|qid=Q9332740|lt=Sagichō|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}}, which is featured annually on NHK.
== Overview == The enshrined deity is '''Akiba Daigongen''' (あきはだいごんげん), who was widely venerated as the deity of fire prevention and suppression within the framework of {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|qid=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. He is said to have originated from the two great sacred sites: the current Enshū Akibayama, represented by {{ill|Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine|en|Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine|qid=Q500413|lt=Akiba Shrine Main Shrine|1=Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine}}<ref group="県" name="栃尾観協">{{Cite web|和書|author=石田哲彌 |title=秋葉神社と秋葉三尺坊 |url=http://tochiokankou.jp/rekishi/akiba.html |website=一般社団法人栃尾観光協会公式ウェブサイト |accessdate=2019-07-29}}</ref>, and {{ill|Jōan-ji|ja|常安寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan-ji|1=Jōan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11481354}}<ref>[https://www.joanji.jp/ 清瀧山 常安寺 公式ホームページ]</ref>. It is said that the Imao Sagichō has been observed since around 1614, when the first lord of the Owari Domain founded Imao Shrine.
== History == It is generally believed that the veneration of Akiba Daigongen spread nationwide after the reign of {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|en|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|qid=Q313745|lt=Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|1=Tokugawa Tsunayoshi}}, but according to extant records, Imao Akiba Shrine (the pilgrimage site of Imao Shrine) was founded in the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|qid=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} era ({{ill|1767|en|1767|qid=Q7672|lt=1767|1=1767}}).
== Events == * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|qid=Q9332740|lt=Sagichō|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} – held on the second Sunday of February At {{ill|Imao Shrine#Imao Sagichō|ja|今尾神社#今尾の左義長|lt=Imao Sagichō|lt_ja=今尾の左義長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Shrine#Imao Sagichō|qq=}}, as detailed below, 13 neighborhood associations<ref group="県" name="gifu1">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/6759.html |title=今尾左義長[いまおさぎちょう] |publisher=岐阜県|date=2015-10-15|accessdate=2024-10-13}}</ref><ref group="海" name="gifu6">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.kaizu.lg.jp/kurashi/cmsfiles/contents/0000001/1553/kubunzu_hirata.pdf|title=平田地区自治会（区）区分図＜地域コミュニティ|format=pdf |publisher=海津市|date=2024-06-28|accessdate=2024-10-13}}</ref> – a few days before the festival, each neighborhood association group of parishioners visits nearby villages to collect over 1,000 bamboo stalks; after temporarily closing local roads, a large bamboo mikoshi is constructed. On the day of the festival, the chief priest and participants proceed from the pilgrimage site at Akiba Shrine, and simultaneously, from each neighborhood, a bamboo mikoshi approximately 7 m in circumference and weighing about 2 t is carried to the plaza of Imao Akiba Shrine amid the parishioners’ chants of “Yoisho, Yoisho” and then ignited, marking the fire festival {{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|qid=Q3135724|lt=Fire Festival|1=Fire Festival}}. The festivities begin in the morning, and by the time the final bamboo mikoshi arrives, darkness has fallen. {{Div col}} * Zaimoku-chō * Kamihon-chō * Nakamachi * Honmachi * Higashi-honmachi * Teramachi * Funamachi * Funato * Shinmachi * Manmachi * Kakunai * Tawaramachi * Shōwa-chō {{Div col end}}
== Proverbs Related to Akiba Shrine == '''Fire from Akiba Mountain''' (あきばさんからかじ) refers to the phenomenon where one in a position to admonish others ends up committing the very misdeeds they warned against<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=新明解故事ことわざ辞典|edition=第二版|year=2016|publisher={{ill|Sanseidō|en|Sanseidō|qid=Q1058813|lt=Sanseido|1=Sanseidō}}|page=7|isbn=978-4385139883}}</ref>.
== Location == ; Location: * {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|Kaizu|en|Kaizu|qid=Q844056|lt=Kaizu City|1=Kaizu}}{{ill|Hirata Town|ja|平田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Town|1=namedachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483353}}Imao
; Transportation Access: * Nearest bus stop: Meihan Kintetsu Bus Kaizu Line – alight at the “Imao” bus stop (1 minute walk)<ref group="県" name="gifu2">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.japan47go.travel/ja/detail/d6415e0b-f79d-4bd6-bc36-22d8bcbae8e3|title=今尾の左義長|publisher=公益社団法人日本観光振興協会|date=2024-03-05|accessdate=2024-10-13}}</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist|group="gm"}} ; Gifu Prefecture Materials: {{Reflist|group="県"}} ; Kaizu City Materials: {{Reflist|group="海"}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite report|和書|author=Yoneya Taisaku|year=2017|url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/235233 |title=The Spread of Akiba Veneration – Focusing on the Distribution of Akiba Shrines –|publisher=Kyoto University Faculty of Letters, Department of Geography|journal=2017 Field Study Report – Hamamatsu City –|pages=153-160|hdl=2433/235233|accessdate=2023-03-28|ref={{harvid|Yoneya (2017)}}}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kaizu|en|Kaizu|qid=Q844056|lt=Kaizu City|1=Kaizu}} * {{ill|Imao Shrine|ja|今尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imao Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Gifu Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties|ja|岐阜県指定文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Gifu Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties|1=list of Gifu Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11471459}} – Intangible Folk Cultural Property * {{ill|Annual Events in Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県内の年中行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Annual Events in Gifu Prefecture|1=Annual Events in Gifu Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11471439}} – February 11
